title: viola canina subsp. stagnina
intro: Viola canina subsp. stagnina, also known as marsh pansy or bog pansy, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the violet family. This perennial plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits associated with Viola canina subsp. stagnina include treating digestive issues, soothing skin irritations, and providing relief from anxiety and stress. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, with the flowers and leaves being used in infusions and the roots being used in decoctions.
